shapes.text:
Saves time and helps you do things faster 
Helps create videos, comics, songs, games 
Improves stories, messages, presentations 
Helps in making projects & school work easier 
Helps you understand & learn new things fast
Helps you think of creative ideas 
Turns your imagination into real creations

shapes.name: Rectangle 4
shapes.text:
Ideas: Helps you start when you don’t know what to write 
Stories: Creates fun stories, plots, and characters 
School work: Helps with essays, answers, and projects 
Grammar: Fixes spelling and sentence mistakes 
Creative writing: Poems, comics, scripts, and speeches 
Improving writing: Makes your writing clearer and more interesting 
Learning: Explains new words and helps you write better over time

shapes.name: Rectangle 7
shapes.text: Use Case 1: AI for Writing

shapes.name: Rectangle 4
shapes.text:
Ideas“Give me 5 fun ideas to write about for a 12-year-old girl who likes ___.”
Stories“Write a short adventure story about a girl from Goa who discovers a secret power. Keep it exciting and age-appropriate.”
School work“Help me write a neat 150-word answer on ___ for class 7. Keep it simple and easy to understand.”
paragraphs: Ideas“Give me 5 fun ideas to write about for a 12-year-old girl who likes ___.”
Stories“Write a short adventure story about a girl from Goa who discovers a secret power. Keep it exciting and age-appropriate.”
School work“Help me write a neat 150-word answer on ___ for class 7. Keep it simple and easy to understand.”

shapes.text:

Creative writing“Write a poem about the beach in Goa with simple words and nice rhymes.”
Improving writing“Make this paragraph more interesting and better, but keep my style:”
Learning“Explain the word ___ with meaning, example, and how I can use it in my writing.”

shapes.name: Title 1
shapes.text: Prompts

shapes.name: Content Placeholder 2
shapes.text:
Context is king!



















5 min video on Mastering Prompts: 
https://academy.openai.com/home/videos/mastering-prompts-the-key-to-getting-what-you-need-from-chatgptmastering-prompts-the-key-to-getting-what-you-need-from-chatgpt-2025-03-20
8 min video on Advanced Prompt engineering: 
https://academy.openai.com/home/videos/advanced-prompt-engineering-2025-02-13
paragraphs: 8 min video on Advanced Prompt engineering:

shapes.name: Content Placeholder 2
shapes.text: Example of a good prompt with role
paragraphs: Example of a good prompt with role

shapes.name: Title 1
shapes.text: How to Write a Great Prompt
paragraphs: How to Write a Great Prompt

shapes.name: Content Placeholder 2
shapes.text:
1. RoleTell AI who it should beExample: “Act like a fun story writer” or “Be my English teacher”

2. ContextExplain your situationExample: “I am a 12-year-old girl from Goa writing a school essay”

3. TaskSay clearly what you wantExample: “Write a 120-word paragraph about my favorite hobby”
paragraphs: 1. RoleTell AI who it should beExample: “Act like a fun story writer” or “Be my English teacher”
2. ContextExplain your situationExample: “I am a 12-year-old girl from Goa writing a school essay”
3. TaskSay clearly what you wantExample: “Write a 120-word paragraph about my favorite hobby”

shapes.name: Title 1
shapes.text: Using AI to improve prompts

shapes.name: Content Placeholder 2
shapes.text:
Custom GPTs
https://chatgpt.com/g/g-684fb793368c819194afae90bab19026-prompt-maker

shapes.name: Content Placeholder 2
shapes.text:
Original prompt. 
I am a freelance corporate behavioral trainer. write an email convincing my potential client to hire my services. 

Improved prompt
Improved Prompt:You are a professional copywriter experienced in B2B communication. 
Write a concise, persuasive cold email for a freelance corporate behavioral trainer trying to secure a contract with a potential client. 
The email should:
Highlight the trainer’s unique value (e.g., customized programs, measurable impact, experience across industries)
Include a compelling subject line
Focus on the client’s possible needs (e.g., team performance, leadership development, communication skills)
End with a clear and professional call to action
Maintain a warm but professional tone suitable for corporate decision-makers
paragraphs: I am a freelance corporate behavioral trainer. write an email convincing my potential client to hire my services.
Improved Prompt:You are a professional copywriter experienced in B2B communication.
Write a concise, persuasive cold email for a freelance corporate behavioral trainer trying to secure a contract with a potential client.
Highlight the trainer’s unique value (e.g., customized programs, measurable impact, experience across industries)
Focus on the client’s possible needs (e.g., team performance, leadership development, communication skills)
End with a clear and professional call to action
Maintain a warm but professional tone suitable for corporate decision-makers

Use Case 2: 
AI for Research Support & Document Summarizing
paragraphs: AI for Research Support & Document Summarizing

shapes.name: Rectangle 7
shapes.text: Use Case 2: Research Help & Summarizing

shapes.name: Rectangle 4
shapes.text:
AI can help with:
 Understand long chapters or lessons 
 Find important points quickly 
 Compare topics 
 Make difficult things easy

shapes.name: Title 1
shapes.text: Demo: AI helps summarize complex info.
paragraphs: Demo: AI helps summarize complex info.

shapes.name: Content Placeholder 2
shapes.text:
Upload a PDF or paste text and ask:
1. Quick Summary (School Chapters)“Summarise this lesson in 5 simple points that a 12-year-old can understand: ___”
2. Homework Help (Long Answers)“Read this paragraph and give me the most important points for my answer: ___”
3. Compare Topics“Explain the difference between ___ and ___ in simple words with examples”
4. Simplify Difficult Text“Explain this in very easy language like you are teaching a student: ___”
5. Notes Making“Turn this chapter into short notes I can revise before exams: ___”
paragraphs: Upload a PDF or paste text and ask:
1. Quick Summary (School Chapters)“Summarise this lesson in 5 simple points that a 12-year-old can understand: ___”
2. Homework Help (Long Answers)“Read this paragraph and give me the most important points for my answer: ___”
3. Compare Topics“Explain the difference between ___ and ___ in simple words with examples”
4. Simplify Difficult Text“Explain this in very easy language like you are teaching a student: ___”
5. Notes Making“Turn this chapter into short notes I can revise before exams: ___”

shapes.name: Title 1
shapes.text: Demo: AI helps compare options and generate ideas
paragraphs: Demo: AI helps compare options and generate ideas

shapes.name: Content Placeholder 2
shapes.text:
1. Compare Options (School Use)“Compare ___ and ___ and tell me the differences, pros, and which is better for a student”
Examples:
“Compare online learning vs classroom learning”
“Compare two sports like football and badminton”

2. Generate Ideas (Creative Thinking)“Give me 10 fun ideas for ___ suitable for a 12-year-old”
Examples:
“Give me 10 ideas for a school project”
“Give me 10 ways to make my class more fun”
“Give me 10 ideas for a story set in Goa”
paragraphs: 1. Compare Options (School Use)“Compare ___ and ___ and tell me the differences, pros, and which is better for a student”
“Compare two sports like football and badminton”
2. Generate Ideas (Creative Thinking)“Give me 10 fun ideas for ___ suitable for a 12-year-old”
“Give me 10 ideas for a school project”
“Give me 10 ways to make my class more fun”
“Give me 10 ideas for a story set in Goa”

shapes.name: Title 1
shapes.text: Deep Research

shapes.name: Content Placeholder 2
shapes.text:
1. Latest Findings (Simple Summary)
Prompt:“Summarise the latest information about ___ in 5–7 simple points that a student can understand”
Examples:
“Summarise the latest trends in AI”
“Summarise new discoveries in space science”

2. Compare Information (Find Similarities & Differences)
Prompt:“Compare different ideas about ___ and tell me where they are similar and where they are different in simple words”
Examples:
“Compare online learning and classroom learning”
“Compare two science topics or inventions”
paragraphs: Prompt:“Summarise the latest information about ___ in 5–7 simple points that a student can understand”
Prompt:“Compare different ideas about ___ and tell me where they are similar and where they are different in simple words”

shapes.name: Title 1
shapes.text: Deep Research

shapes.name: Content Placeholder 2
shapes.text:
3. Turn Learning into Action (Very Powerful)
Prompt:“Turn this information about ___ into simple steps I can follow in real life or school”
Examples:
“Turn tips on time management into steps I can follow daily”
“Turn study techniques into a daily routine”
paragraphs: Prompt:“Turn this information about ___ into simple steps I can follow in real life or school”
“Turn tips on time management into steps I can follow daily”
“Turn study techniques into a daily routine”

shapes.name: Rectangle 7
shapes.text:
Use Case 3: 
AI for Presentations & Videos

shapes.name: Rectangle 7
shapes.text: Use Case 3: AI for Presentations

shapes.name: Rectangle 4
shapes.text:
How to use AI for making presentations
Tool demo: Chatgpt (with Canva App) / Claude.ai / Gamma 
Live example: Turn text into a professional-looking powerpoint presentation in minutes

Quick workflow (3 steps)
-Ask AI for a slide outline and speaker notes
-Use Claude.ai or Canva app in chatgpt.com or paste output into Gamma and choose a template
-Edit local examples, add organisation logo, and export
paragraphs: How to use AI for making presentations
Tool demo: Chatgpt (with Canva App) / Claude.ai / Gamma
Live example: Turn text into a professional-looking powerpoint presentation in minutes
-Ask AI for a slide outline and speaker notes
-Use Claude.ai or Canva app in chatgpt.com or paste output into Gamma and choose a template
-Edit local examples, add organisation logo, and export

shapes.name: Rectangle 4
shapes.text: Gamma.app

shapes.name: Rectangle 7
shapes.text: Use Case 3: AI for Presentations

shapes.name: Rectangle 4
shapes.text:
Demo 1 – Create Slides from a Topic
Prompt:“Create a 5-slide presentation on ___ for a school student. For each slide give a title and 3 simple bullet points”
Examples:
“Create a 5-slide presentation on road safety”
“Create a 5-slide presentation on saving the environment”
paragraphs: Demo 1 – Create Slides from a Topic
Prompt:“Create a 5-slide presentation on ___ for a school student. For each slide give a title and 3 simple bullet points”
“Create a 5-slide presentation on saving the environment”

shapes.name: Rectangle 7
shapes.text: Use Case 3: AI for Presentations

shapes.name: Rectangle 4
shapes.text:
Demo 2 – Turn Text into Slides
Prompt:“Convert this paragraph into 4–5 slides. Give each slide a title and 3 simple points: ___”
👉 Great for turning textbook answers into presentations

Demo 3 – School Topic Presentation
Prompt:“Create a 5-slide presentation on ___ with simple language suitable for class 6–8 students”
Examples: 
“Healthy habits for students”, 
“My favorite festival”
paragraphs: Prompt:“Convert this paragraph into 4–5 slides. Give each slide a title and 3 simple points: ___”
👉 Great for turning textbook answers into presentations
Prompt:“Create a 5-slide presentation on ___ with simple language suitable for class 6–8 students”

Making Videos using AI

Stories, Short reels, Funny or creative videos 
⚡ Demo Prompt 
“Create a 1-minute fun video script where 5 kids in Goa discover a secret power. Include scenes, dialogues, and a twist ending.”

Music: Suno.com
paragraphs: AI can be used to create videos for:
Stories, Short reels, Funny or creative videos
“Create a 1-minute fun video script where 5 kids in Goa discover a secret power. Include scenes, dialogues, and a twist ending.”

shapes.name: Rectangle 7
shapes.text:
Reya: A Minecraft warrior skating through pixel world
Saily: A Disney princess reading in an enchanted library
Minty: Hermione casting spells at Hogwarts
Arnav: Iron Man building a Lego Avengers tower
Daksh: Tintin & Snowy discovering a hidden temple
paragraphs: Reya: A Minecraft warrior skating through pixel world
Saily: A Disney princess reading in an enchanted library
Minty: Hermione casting spells at Hogwarts
Arnav: Iron Man building a Lego Avengers tower
Daksh: Tintin & Snowy discovering a hidden temple

shapes.name: Rectangle 7
shapes.text:
Activity: “Create Your Own Mini Movie with AI”

⚡ Step 1 – Choose Their Theme (based on interests)
Minecraft Adventure (Reya) 
Sports Champion Story (Daksh / Saily) 
Magic World Story (Minty – inspired by Harry Potter) 
Superhero Mission (Arnav – inspired by The Avengers) 
Real-life Business Story (Arti – “Fashion shop becomes famous!”)
paragraphs: Activity: “Create Your Own Mini Movie with AI”
⚡ Step 1 – Choose Their Theme (based on interests)
Magic World Story (Minty – inspired by Harry Potter)
Superhero Mission (Arnav – inspired by The Avengers)
Real-life Business Story (Arti – “Fashion shop becomes famous!”)

shapes.name: Rectangle 7
shapes.text:
Step 2 – Power Prompt
master prompt:
“Create a 1-minute fun video script for kids where the main characters are Reya, Saily, Minty, Arnav, and Daksh.Include their interests like Minecraft, badminton, Harry Potter-style magic, superheroes, and karate.Make it exciting with scenes, dialogues, and a twist ending.
paragraphs: “Create a 1-minute fun video script for kids where the main characters are Reya, Saily, Minty, Arnav, and Daksh.Include their interests like Minecraft, badminton, Harry Potter-style magic, superheroes, and karate.Make it exciting with scenes, dialogues, and a twist ending.

shapes.name: Rectangle 7
shapes.text:
Step 3 – Make It Even Cooler
Each of you can upgrade it:
“Add a villain” 
“Give each character a special power” 
“Make it funny” 
“Add a Goa setting (beach, school, etc.)”
paragraphs: Each of you can upgrade it:

shapes.name: Rectangle 7
shapes.text:
Step 4 – Add Music
Use Suno
Prompt:“Create energetic background music for a fun kids adventure video”

Bonus Challenge (for Arti + older kids)
“Turn this into a short Instagram reel idea with captions and scenes”

Final Demo Idea (Group Activity)
Split into 2 teams:
Team 1: Superhero + Magic 
Team 2: Sports + Real Life
paragraphs: Prompt:“Create energetic background music for a fun kids adventure video”
“Turn this into a short Instagram reel idea with captions and scenes”

shapes.name: Title 1
shapes.text: Use Case: Create Your Own Comic Book with AI
paragraphs: Use Case: Create Your Own Comic Book with AI

shapes.name: Content Placeholder 2
shapes.text:
Step 1 – Choose Their Style (based on interests)
Minecraft-style world (Reya)
Magic + fantasy (Minty – Harry Potter vibe)
Sports hero story (Daksh / Saily)
Superhero action (Arnav – The Avengers vibe)
Real-life hero story (Arti – business + problem solving)

Step 2 – Super Comic Prompt
“Create a 6-panel comic book story for kids with characters Reya, Saily, Minty, Arnav, and Daksh.Each character should have a unique power based on their interests.Include dialogues, a villain, and a fun twist ending.”
paragraphs: Step 1 – Choose Their Style (based on interests)
Real-life hero story (Arti – business + problem solving)

shapes.name: Title 1
shapes.text: Use Case: Create Your Own Game with AI
paragraphs: Use Case: Create Your Own Game with AI

shapes.name: Content Placeholder 2
shapes.text:
1. Reaction Game
Prompt: “Create a simple web game in one HTML file where a box appears randomly and I click it to score. Add score and 20-second timer.”

2. Memory Card Game
Prompt: “Create a memory card matching game in one HTML file with a 4x4 grid. Add move counter and restart button.”

3. Dodge Game
Prompt: “Create a simple web game in one HTML file where I move a square with arrow keys and avoid falling blocks. Add score and game over.”
paragraphs: Prompt: “Create a simple web game in one HTML file where a box appears randomly and I click it to score. Add score and 20-second timer.”
Prompt: “Create a memory card matching game in one HTML file with a 4x4 grid. Add move counter and restart button.”
Prompt: “Create a simple web game in one HTML file where I move a square with arrow keys and avoid falling blocks. Add score and game over.”

2. Space Shooter Game

3. Breakout Game
Prompt: “Create a breakout web game in one HTML file with paddle, ball and bricks. Add score and game over.”

shapes.name: Rectangle 7
shapes.text:
Use Case 4: 
AI for Spreadsheet Creation & Data Analysis
paragraphs: AI for Spreadsheet Creation & Data Analysis

shapes.name: Title 1
shapes.text: Create spreadsheets using AI

shapes.name: Content Placeholder 2
shapes.text:
1. Study Tracker
Prompt: “Create a simple spreadsheet to track my daily study. Add subject, time, and total study hours.”

2. Game Score Tracker
Prompt: “Create a spreadsheet to track scores for 5 players. Add total score and show the winner.”

3. Sports Tracker
Prompt: “Create a badminton practice tracker with date, practice time, and average performance.”

4. Small Business Tracker (Arti angle)
Prompt: “Create a simple sales tracker for a clothes shop with total sales and profit.”
paragraphs: Prompt: “Create a simple spreadsheet to track my daily study. Add subject, time, and total study hours.”
Prompt: “Create a spreadsheet to track scores for 5 players. Add total score and show the winner.”
Prompt: “Create a badminton practice tracker with date, practice time, and average performance.”
Prompt: “Create a simple sales tracker for a clothes shop with total sales and profit.”

shapes.name: Title 1
shapes.text: Creating a spreadsheet from a simple  idea Step by Step
paragraphs: Creating a spreadsheet from a simple  idea Step by Step

shapes.name: Content Placeholder 2
shapes.text:
Step 1 – Start Simple
Prompt: “Create a simple spreadsheet to track my daily study”

Step 2 – AI gives structure
You’ll get columns like:Date | Subject | Time Studied | Homework Done

Step 3 – Improve it
Prompt: “Add total study time and highlight days where I studied less than 1 hour”

Step 4 – Add Charts (cool part)
Prompt: “Add a chart to show how my study time is changing over days”

Step 5 – Make it look awesome
Prompt: “Make this spreadsheet colorful and easy to understand”
paragraphs: Prompt: “Create a simple spreadsheet to track my daily study”
You’ll get columns like:Date | Subject | Time Studied | Homework Done
Prompt: “Add total study time and highlight days where I studied less than 1 hour”
Prompt: “Add a chart to show how my study time is changing over days”
Prompt: “Make this spreadsheet colorful and easy to understand”

shapes.name: Rectangle 7
shapes.text: Demo: Analyse operational data & identify trends
paragraphs: Demo: Analyse operational data & identify trends

shapes.name: Rectangle 4
shapes.text:
Summarise the key trends from this operational data and suggest 3 actions management should take to improve performance.

Create a chart-based dashboard (bar chart, line chart, heatmap) 

Identify any patterns in this monthly production data. Explain the trends in simple language.
paragraphs: Summarise the key trends from this operational data and suggest 3 actions management should take to improve performance.
Create a chart-based dashboard (bar chart, line chart, heatmap)
Identify any patterns in this monthly production data. Explain the trends in simple language.

shapes.name: Rectangle 4
shapes.text:
Key Trends from the Data
Production & Revenue — All four divisions grew steadily through FY 2024, with the sharpest acceleration in Q4. Division A dominates output (607 units, ₹30.1L) while Division C is the smallest but most efficient producer. The revenue trend lines show no major stumbles — healthy, broad-based growth.
Defect Rate — This is the clearest red flag. Division D's defect rate averages 3.59%, which is more than three times Division C's 1.11%. Importantly, the heatmap reveals that July is the worst month for defects across nearly every division — the same month on-time delivery also collapses. These two patterns almost certainly have a common cause.
On-Time Delivery — Division C leads at 95.8% average; Division D trails at just 84.75%. The July dip is visible in every division simultaneously, which rules out a single-division explanation — this is a company-wide operational disruption that repeats annually.
Machine Utilisation — All divisions improved through the year, but Division C runs at only 64.8% utilisation despite its superior quality numbers. That's untapped capacity that management can exploit without buying new equipment.
paragraphs: Production & Revenue — All four divisions grew steadily through FY 2024, with the sharpest acceleration in Q4. Division A dominates output (607 units, ₹30.1L) while Division C is the smallest but most efficient producer. The revenue trend lines show no major stumbles — healthy, broad-based growth.

Defect Rate — This is the clearest red flag. Division D's defect rate averages 3.59%, which is more than three times Division C's 1.11%. Importantly, the heatmap reveals that July is the worst month for defects across nearly every division — the same month on-time delivery also collapses. These two patterns almost certainly have a common cause.

On-Time Delivery — Division C leads at 95.8% average; Division D trails at just 84.75%. The July dip is visible in every division simultaneously, which rules out a single-division explanation — this is a company-wide operational disruption that repeats annually.

Machine Utilisation — All divisions improved through the year, but Division C runs at only 64.8% utilisation despite its superior quality numbers. That's untapped capacity that management can exploit without buying new equipment.

shapes.name: Rectangle 4
shapes.text:
Management Actions to Improve Performance
Action 01 · Quality
Fix Division D's defect problem
Division D averages a 3.59% defect rate — 3× higher than Division C. Conduct a root-cause analysis: inspect tooling, operator training, and incoming material quality. A 1% reduction in defects could unlock significant cost savings and protect customer satisfaction.

Action 02 · Capacity
Unlock Division C's idle machines
Division C runs at only 64.8% machine utilisation — the lowest of all divisions — despite having the best quality and delivery scores. Investigate downtime causes (maintenance, scheduling gaps) and push utilisation toward 75–80% to grow output without capital investment.

Action 03 · Reliability
Investigate the July delivery slump
On-time delivery drops in July across every single division — not just one. This is a systemic signal, not a local issue. Likely causes include planned maintenance shutdowns, monsoon-related logistics delays, or mid-year shift in workforce. A pre-July readiness plan should be put in place for FY 2025.
paragraphs: Division D averages a 3.59% defect rate — 3× higher than Division C. Conduct a root-cause analysis: inspect tooling, operator training, and incoming material quality. A 1% reduction in defects could unlock significant cost savings and protect customer satisfaction.

Division C runs at only 64.8% machine utilisation — the lowest of all divisions — despite having the best quality and delivery scores. Investigate downtime causes (maintenance, scheduling gaps) and push utilisation toward 75–80% to grow output without capital investment.

On-time delivery drops in July across every single division — not just one. This is a systemic signal, not a local issue. Likely causes include planned maintenance shutdowns, monsoon-related logistics delays, or mid-year shift in workforce. A pre-July readiness plan should be put in place for FY 2025.

shapes.name: Rectangle 4
shapes.text:
Management Actions to Improve Performance

The 3 Actions in brief:

Fix Division D quality — root-cause the 3.59% defect rate; it's eroding margins and customer trust.

Unlock Division C's capacity — nudge utilisation from 65% to 75–80% for free volume gains.

Pre-empt the July slump — it affects every metric, every division, every year — likely a structural operational issue (monsoon logistics, maintenance windows, or staffing).
paragraphs: Fix Division D quality — root-cause the 3.59% defect rate; it's eroding margins and customer trust.
Unlock Division C's capacity — nudge utilisation from 65% to 75–80% for free volume gains.
Pre-empt the July slump — it affects every metric, every division, every year — likely a structural operational issue (monsoon logistics, maintenance windows, or staffing).

shapes.name: Rectangle 7
shapes.text: Best Practices for AI Use, Safety
paragraphs: Best Practices for AI Use, Safety

shapes.name: Rectangle 4
shapes.text:
Don’t share personal info
Passwords or Photos which you don’t want to go public

2. Check before you trust
AI can be wrong sometimes👉 Always double check

3. Use your brain
AI helps you:
Write
Learn
create
But you decide what is right
paragraphs: Passwords or Photos which you don’t want to go public
AI can be wrong sometimes👉 Always double check
But you decide what is right

shapes.name: Rectangle 7
shapes.text: Safety Guidelines

shapes.name: Rectangle 4
shapes.text:
❌ What NOT to Share
Personal info (phone number, school details, passwords)
Private chats (WhatsApp, messages)
Confidential documents or important files

✔ What is Safe to Share
Made-up (fictional) examples
Your own notes
School questions and projects
General topics (like sports, stories, science)

Ask yourself
“Would I be okay if this was seen by everyone?”
If NO → Don’t share 👍
paragraphs: Personal info (phone number, school details, passwords)
“Would I be okay if this was seen by everyone?”

shapes.name: Rectangle 7
shapes.text: AI Mistakes (Hallucinations)

shapes.name: Rectangle 4
shapes.text:
What does it mean?
Sometimes AI gives an answer that sounds correct… but is actually wrong or made up

Why does this happen?
The question is not clear
AI doesn’t have enough information
It tries to guess instead of saying “I don’t know”

Why is this important?
You may learn something wrong
You may use incorrect info in school work
It can confuse you
paragraphs: Sometimes AI gives an answer that sounds correct… but is actually wrong or made up
It tries to guess instead of saying “I don’t know”
You may use incorrect info in school work

shapes.name: Rectangle 7
shapes.text: How to Avoid AI Hallucinations

shapes.name: Rectangle 4
shapes.text:
Use Thinking mode for step by step reasoning

2. Double check
Use: Textbooks, Teachers, Trusted websites

3. Don’t blindly copy
If you’re not sure → don’t use it
paragraphs: Use Thinking mode for step by step reasoning
If you’re not sure → don’t use it

I'm experiencing the following challenge at work:[DETAILED PROBLEM DESCRIPTION]. The context is: [WORKPLACE CONTEXT]. I've already tried [PREVIOUS SOLUTION ATTEMPTS] without success.

(4) Metrics to evaluate whether the solution is working.
